---
title: "Data Extraction"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---


```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *
import polars.selectors as cs


LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```


```{python}
df = pl.read_csv(
    '../gpu_database.csv',
	null_values=['null', 'NULL', 'None', ''],
	infer_schema_length=10000,
	schema_overrides={'cuda_cores': pl.Int64}
)
``` 



```{python}
used_price_by_category = (
    df.filter(pl.col('price_usd_ebay_used').is_not_null())
    .group_by('category')
    .agg(pl.len().alias('used_price_count'))
    .sort('used_price_count', descending=True)
)

# used_price_by_category

```

```{python include=false}
# Filter for used eBay prices, exclude mobile category
df_plot = df.filter(
    (pl.col('price_usd_ebay_used').is_not_null()) & 
    (pl.col('category') != 'mobile')
).select([
    'gpu_name', 'vram_gb', 'price_usd_ebay_used'
])

# Row counts for used price data and plot eligibility
used_price_rows = df.filter(pl.col('price_usd_ebay_used').is_not_null()).height
used_price_non_mobile_rows = df.filter(
    (pl.col('price_usd_ebay_used').is_not_null()) &
    (pl.col('category') != 'mobile')
).height

missing_vram_rows = df_plot.filter(pl.col('vram_gb').is_null()).height
missing_name_rows = df_plot.filter(pl.col('gpu_name').is_null()).height

plot_ready_rows = df_plot.filter(
    (pl.col('vram_gb').is_not_null()) &
    (pl.col('gpu_name').is_not_null()) &
    (pl.col('price_usd_ebay_used').is_not_null())
).height

# debug prints hidden for clean output
# print(f"Rows with used price (all categories): {used_price_rows}")
# print(f"Rows with used price (non-mobile): {used_price_non_mobile_rows}")
# print(f"Rows eligible for plot (non-null vram/name/price): {plot_ready_rows}")
# if missing_vram_rows > 0 or missing_name_rows > 0:
#     print(
#         f"Rows dropped due to missing data -> vram_gb: {missing_vram_rows}, gpu_name: {missing_name_rows}"
#     )

```

```{python echo=false}
# Create scatter plot: VRAM vs Used Price, colored by GPU name
plot = (
    ggplot(df_plot, aes(x='vram_gb', y='price_usd_ebay_used', color='gpu_name')) +
    geom_point(size=2.5, alpha=0.7, tooltips=layer_tooltips().title("@gpu_name"), show_legend=False) +

    scale_x_continuous(limits=[0, 32]) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits=[0, 1000]) +
    labs(
        title='GPU Used Price vs VRAM',
        x='VRAM (GB)', 
        y='Used Price - eBay (USD)',
        color='GPU'
    ) +
    theme_gray()
)
plot

# save_plot(plot, 'gpu_price_plot.png')  # Then save it
```

```{python}

# VRAM amount vs VRAM type scatter (colored by GPU name)
# Exclude noisy or irrelevant VRAM type labels here
vram_exclude = [
    'unknown', 'shared', 'HBM?', 'other', 'Unified LPDDR5X', 'Unified LPDDR5', 'Unified LPDDR4X', 'SDRAM', 'SDR', 'DDR', 'DDR2', 'GDDR2', 'GDDR4'
]

df_vram = df.filter(
    (pl.col('vram_gb').is_not_null()) &
    (pl.col('vram_type').is_not_null()) &
    (~pl.col('vram_type').is_in(vram_exclude)) &
    (pl.col('category') != 'mobile') &
    (pl.col('gpu_name').is_not_null())
).select(['gpu_name', 'vram_gb', 'vram_type'])

plot_vram = (
    ggplot(df_vram, aes(x='vram_type', y='vram_gb', color='gpu_name')) +
    geom_jitter(width=0.2, height=0, size=2.5, alpha=0.8, tooltips=layer_tooltips().title("@gpu_name"), show_legend=False) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits=[4, 128]) +
    labs(
        title='GPU VRAM Type and Amount',
        x='VRAM Type',
        y='VRAM (GB)'
    ) +
    theme_gray()
)
plot_vram


```
